name: Vegetable Tempura
recipeIngredient: Vegetables: carrots, broccoli, cauliflower, potatoes, bell pepper, parsnips, etc.
Unbleached flour for dredging the vegetables
Cooking oil for deep-frying the tempura vegetables
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all of the dry ingredients together in a large bowl.
2. Gradually add water while stirring until the mixture has the consistency of a light batter.
3. The batter can be used immediately or refrigerated until needed. Leftover batter can be saved for future use.
4. Cut the vegetables into manageable pieces:
- Broccoli and cauliflower into florets
- Carrots, potatoes, and parsnips into slices about 3/8 inch thick
- Bell peppers into strips about 1/2 inch wide
5. Dredge each vegetable piece in unbleached flour, shaking off excess.
6. Heat cooking oil (about 375°F) in a deep frying pan.
7. Dip the dredged vegetable pieces into the tempura batter to coat thoroughly.
8. Carefully lower the coated vegetables into the hot oil and fry until golden brown.
9. Remove with tongs or a slotted spoon, drain on paper towels, and serve immediately.
10. Serve with a dipping sauce such as sweet and sour sauce.
